text: I feel a mix of curiosity and dislocation. The glass meeting room, sterile and faintly illuminated, gives me an almost clinical sense of separation—from others, from events, from myself. Moving to the vivid display of "R+D," I am struck by the contrast: bright colors and layered information evoke a sense of creativity and possibility. Yet, the environment still feels orchestrated and slightly unreal, as though the line between observation and participation blurs. In this space of research and development, anticipation mingles with a quiet uncertainty about what is truly being created—or discovered.
